channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Senate Banking panel advances stablecoin bill
item.description: The Senate Banking Committee voted Thursday to advanced legislation to create a regulatory framework for payment stablecoins.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This volunteer crew is on a mission to fix Wikipedia’s worst celebrity photos
item.description: Visit a celebrity’s Wikipedia page and there’s a good chance you’ll be greeted by a blurry, outdated, or unflattering photo. These images often look like they were snapped in passing at a public event—because, in many cases, they were.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco’s chief product officer explains the company’s transformation around AI
item.description: For 40 years, Cisco has been best known for building routers, switches, and other networking technology that connects computers within offices, data centers, and across the internet.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Intel hires new CEO in struggling chipmaker’s latest comeback attempt
item.description: Intel’s previous CEO, Pat Gelsinger, abruptly retired last year amid a deepening downturn that triggered massive layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: VergeIO enhances VergeFabric network virtualization offering
item.description: VergeIO is looking to shake up the virtual infrastructure market with its approach to virtualization and software-defined networking. VergeIO is not a startup; the company was founded in 2010 and hasn’t made huge waves in the market.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ducks were once a conservation bright spot. Now they're declining in the US, new report shows
item.description: A new report says at least 112 North American bird species have lost more than half their populations in the past 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Samsung's March Madness TV Bundle: Eight Premium TVs for Ultimate Sports Viewing Experience
item.description: Samsung's Buzzer Beater Bundle offers eight premium TVs perfect for March Madness viewing experience.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: An inside look at Las Vegas valley’s first AI-powered hotel
item.description: Otonomous Hotel, under construction at Russell and Decatur, uses machine learning to create a personalized experience for guests.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, NewsNation

item.title: Today’s Steam Spring Sale features my absolute favorite game of all time - here's when the sale starts and all the key info
item.description: Here's the Steam Spring Sale 2025 start times for your timezone, and some predictions as to what we'd like to see discounted.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Scopes monkey trial took place 100 years ago, but the fight isn't over
item.description: Tennessee became the first state in the country a hundred years ago to ban the teaching of evolution in public school classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft faces FTC antitrust probe over AI and licensing practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) is reportedly pressing ahead with an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, a move that could reshape competition in AI and productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices but US remains hesitant
item.description: Vaccines could be a key means of suppressing bird flu and avoiding the slaughter of millions of chickens, which is blamed for egg prices averaging nearly $5 a dozen.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google unveils Gemma 3 multi-modal AI models
item.description: Google DeepMind has introduced Gemma 3, an update to the company’s family of generative AI models, featuring multi-modality that allows the models to analyze images, answer questions about images, identify objects, and perform other tasks that involve analyzing and understanding visual data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Johnson says Congress will investigate attacks against Tesla owners, vehicles
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Wednesday that Congress will investigate attacks against Tesla vehicles and their owners as Elon Musk’s flagship electric vehicle company faces mounting backlash over its CEO’s controversial role in the Trump administration’s cost-cutting initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 2 tasty new tab tricks for Chrome on Android
item.description: Quick: How many tabs do you have open in Chrome on your Android phone right now? If the answer is zero, you’re an animal. Seek immediate psychiatric help. If the answer is one to two, you’re obsessively organized and a model Android device owner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk’s White House role weighs on business empire
item.description: Elon Musk’s role in the Trump administration is complicating his business ventures, as the tech billionaire pushes forward with efforts to slash the federal government and budget in the face of mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: JavaOne 2025: Oracle's Annual Conference Celebrates 30 Years of Java
item.description: Oracle to hold JavaOne conference, marking 30 years of Java with a focus on platform evolution, generative AI, and machine learning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dangerous malware targets you through fake loan app
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a malware app called SpyLend is posing as a financial tool, hitting 100,000-plus downloads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: IBM wins UK lawsuit against LzLabs for mainframe intellectual property theft
item.description: When it comes to protecting its mainframe technology, IBM wields a pretty big sword. This week, its defense triumphed as IBM won a judgement against LzLabs for violating Big Blue’s intellectual property rights.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Helping DOGE cut waste
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sovereign European Cloud API claims to offer interoperability without lock-in
item.description: Europe’s efforts to free itself from the domination of US cloud platforms have reached an important first milestone with the announcement of the Sovereign European Cloud API (SECA).
item.dc:creator: Network World
